author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Added first-class ufunc objects in new ferray-ufunc/src/ufunc_object.rs module. The Ufunc struct wraps a binary op plus an identity element and exposes the NumPy ufunc methods (.call, .reduce, .accumulate, .outer, .at). All five delegate to the generic machinery already built for issue #381, so no duplicated traversal code.

Pre-registered constructors for the common NumPy names: add_ufunc, subtract_ufunc, multiply_ufunc, divide_ufunc — each returns a fresh Ufunc with the correct identity (0 for add/subtract, 1 for multiply/divide).

User-defined ufuncs work in one line: Ufunc::new("max", f64::NEG_INFINITY, f64::max). The struct is Copy so callers can pass it around freely.

Existing free functions (add, add_reduce, multiply_outer, etc.) are unchanged — ufunc_object is purely additive and composes with everything already in place.

11 new unit tests cover: elementwise call, reduce (1D and 2D row sums), accumulate (including running differences), outer product, unbuffered scatter-reduce with duplicate indices, the user-defined max example from the issue, divide, and the Copy contract. Plus 1 new doctest showing the basic API. 255 lib tests (up from 244) + 50/19/53 integration tests pass. Clippy clean on the full workspace.
